reforms.description: Portugal's Recovery and Resilience Plan includes initiatives aimed at supporting the digitalisation of domestic firms, with EUR 650 million allocated to enhance the innovation capacity of small and medium-sized enterprises through digital skills training and financial incentives. Additionally, the plan aims to simplify licensing procedures to address housing shortages, which indirectly supports the startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The initiatives are expected to enhance productivity and innovation among SMEs, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiatives are part of the announced Recovery and Resilience Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms involve significant investment and structural changes aimed at improving the innovation ecosystem for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The Recovery and Resilience Plan foresees EUR 650 million to support small and medium-sized enterprises and their workers through digital skills training and financial incentives to adopt digital technologies.

reforms.description: Portugal's Recovery and Resilience Plan includes significant investments aimed at strengthening research and promoting innovation, with a budget allocation of EUR 2.9 billion. Additionally, there is an increase in direct R&D support targeted at SMEs to enhance their innovation capacity and overall productivity.
reforms.component_name: R&D Funding and Support for SMEs
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The substantial funding is expected to bolster research activities and innovation, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding initiatives are part of the announced Recovery and Resilience Plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a major investment in public R&D funding, which is critical for enhancing the innovation landscape.
reforms.source_quote: Strengthening research and promoting innovation and investment (EUR 2.9 billion).

reforms.description: The government offers generous tax credits for business R&D expenditures, particularly benefiting small firms that are more responsive to such incentives. Unused tax credits can be carried forward for eight years, which is crucial for young innovative firms that may not be profitable initially.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credits are designed to stimulate R&D investment among firms, particularly small businesses, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit scheme is currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform provides significant incentives for R&D investment, which is important for enhancing innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: To foster innovative activities, the government offers generous tax credits to business R&D expenditures and a preferential tax treatment to small firms.

reforms.description: The planned grants for the implementation of joint programmes between higher education institutions, firms, and other stakeholders aim to foster greater networks and collaboration, enhancing the alignment of graduates' skills with industry demand.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning educational outcomes with industry needs, the initiative is expected to enhance the employability of graduates and support innovation in the workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The grants for joint programmes have been planned and announced.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform aims to improve collaboration between academia and industry, which is significant for enhancing human capital.
reforms.source_quote: The planned grants for the implementation of joint programmes between higher education institutions, firms and other stakeholders for new postgraduate schools, programmes and alliances could be one step to help to foster greater networks.

reforms.description: Portugal plans to invest EUR 466 million in primary health care services by mid-2026, which includes building new primary care centres and refurbishing existing ones to enhance service capacity. Additionally, there is an investment in new hardware and software for health workers to standardize public information systems and improve access to health data, aiming to enhance digital healthcare technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investments are expected to improve healthcare services and efficiency, contributing positively to public health and economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The investment plans have been announced as part of the health care reform strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant investment in health infrastructure, which is critical for improving healthcare delivery.
reforms.source_quote: The welcome investments planned under the RRP notably include new hardware and software for health workers, the standardisation of the public information systems and better access to data.
